10-your-30-60-90-180-day-plan/01-how-to-.label: Your first 90 Days as a Growth Marketer: A Practical Blueprint
How much time can you honestly give this plan each week?

10-your-30-60-90-180-day-plan/02-day-0-c.label: Day 0 — commitments
items: Name the growth owner — one person accountable for measure → keep → grow (a hat, not a hire)
Write the activation sentence: the one user action that means 'got real value', in one sentence
Get the team to agree the order out loud: retention before acquisition
Book the weekly 30-minute growth review, recurring, starting next week
Pick where growth work lives: the same backlog/repo as product work, not a separate doc nobody opens

10-your-30-60-90-180-day-plan/02-day-0-c.label: Who owns growth? Name the person (and write it like it's a real assignment, because it is).
10-your-30-60-90-180-day-plan/02-day-0-c.placeholder: Growth owner: … — accountable for the funnel being instrumented, the dashboard being read, and the journeys being live.

10-your-30-60-90-180-day-plan/02-day-0-c.label: Your activation sentence — if you drafted it in Chapter 2, it's pre-filled here. Make it final.
10-your-30-60-90-180-day-plan/02-day-0-c.placeholder: A user is activated when they… (one sentence, one observable event)

10-your-30-60-90-180-day-plan/03-days-1-.label: Days 1–30 — Measure
items: PostHog account on the right region; one project; website + app tracked together
posthog-js installed with identified_only; identify at login, reset at logout
Starter event kit firing — signup client-side; subscription + payment server-side
Tracking plan written down and shared — even ten rows in a doc beats zero
Your one activation event defined, instrumented, and confirmed firing
Managed reverse proxy set up so tracking survives ad-blockers
Daily dashboard built and pinned: North Star, activation funnel, retention, alerts → Slack
First data QA pass: watch 5 session replays and confirm the events match what you see

10-your-30-60-90-180-day-plan/04-days-31.label: Days 31–60 — Keep
items: Hogsend app scaffolded; backend wired to POST /v1/ingest
Welcome + onboarding journey live, triggered by the signup event
Activation nudge at +48h, skipped automatically if the activation event fired
Dunning journey live (if you charge) — failed payment → retry prompt sequence
Win-back journey live for users inactive 14+ days
A holdout on your first journey, and you know how to read its lift in PostHog
Global frequency cap set so overlapping journeys can't spam one user
Unsubscribe/preferences verified working end-to-end (send yourself every email)

10-your-30-60-90-180-day-plan/05-days-61.label: Your First 90 Days as Head of Growth (The Exact Plan I Give Every Client)
Days 61–90 — Grow
items: Channel decision made deliberately: is paid even your channel, or is it content/launches/community? (Ch 7)
If paid: Meta Pixel + PostHog → Meta CAPI destination, same Pixel ID, deduped, EMQ ≥ 6
One campaign live, optimising the deepest conversion event you can feed ~50/week
Consent captured at email collection; no dark-pattern pre-ticks
Discord and/or Telegram connector live with the /link email-verify flow (Ch 8)
Linking perk journey live — linked identity gets something real within a minute
Referral ask placed at the aha milestone, not before; verified referrals rewarded
Verified emails synced as a custom audience + lookalike seed

10-your-30-60-90-180-day-plan/05-days-61.label: Can you afford to open the tap?

10-your-30-60-90-180-day-plan/06-days-91.label: Days 91–180 — Compound
items: Experiment backlog exists, ICE-scored, groomed in the weekly growth review
Steady test tempo: every week at least one experiment launches or reports
Every experiment ships with a hypothesis, a primary metric, and a written result — including the losers
Global holdout on the lifecycle program; incremental lift reported monthly
Blended CAC reviewed monthly, with the referral loop's contribution broken out
PMF check run (40% test) before any major step-up in paid spend
Quarterly prune: kill journeys with no lift, events nobody queries, ad sets that don't convert
The activation metric re-validated — still the best predictor of retention you have?

10-your-30-60-90-180-day-plan/08-before-.label: When are you actually starting this plan?
The ultimate guide to A/B testing

10-your-30-60-90-180-day-plan/09-zero-to.label: Zero-to-one — before the day-0 clock starts
items: Talked to at least ten real potential users about the problem, not the product
Defined the one activation event that would mean 'they got real value', and instrumented it
Onboarded the first users by hand — personally, unscalably — and watched them use it
At least a few users would be 'very disappointed' to lose it (the seed of the 40% test)
The tap is still off: no paid acquisition until early retention shows a pulse

10-your-30-60-90-180-day-plan/09-zero-to.label: What's the single most important thing you need to learn before you're ready to instrument and run the full plan?
10-your-30-60-90-180-day-plan/09-zero-to.placeholder: The riskiest assumption I need evidence on is… — I'll know it's answered when…

10-your-30-60-90-180-day-plan/10-which-p.label: Which stage are you actually in right now?
options: Pre-launch — no product in users' hands yet
Just launched — a handful of users
Early traction — users and some retention
Scaling — a channel that works and budget to spend

10-your-30-60-90-180-day-plan/11-role-ba.label: Who owns this plan, and which phase will be hardest for them? Name the support that de-risks it.
10-your-30-60-90-180-day-plan/11-role-ba.placeholder: Owner: … — the hardest phase for them is … — I'll de-risk it by …

10-your-30-60-90-180-day-plan/12-the-wee.label: Weekly growth review — the standing agenda
items: North Star + the two or three input metrics beneath it: up, flat, or down since last week?
The one gate we're working toward (day 30/60/90/180) — are we closer?
Experiments: what launched, what's reading, what shipped or got killed (including the losers)
One anomaly worth a look — a replay to watch, an alert that fired, a cohort that dropped
Next week's single most important growth action, and who owns it
Anything blocking the person who owns growth this week

10-your-30-60-90-180-day-plan/12-the-wee.label: What's the standing question your weekly review opens with — the one that points the team at the goal that matters right now?
10-your-30-60-90-180-day-plan/12-the-wee.placeholder: Our review always starts by asking… (e.g. 'Is the bucket holding water yet?')

10-your-30-60-90-180-day-plan/13-the-exp.label: Draft your next experiment in the template: hypothesis, primary metric + guardrails, how long it runs, and how you'll decide.
10-your-30-60-90-180-day-plan/13-the-exp.placeholder: Hypothesis: We believe … for … will cause … because … · Primary metric: … · Guardrails: … · Runs until: … · Decision rule: …

10-your-30-60-90-180-day-plan/14-after-1.label: What's the single next constraint on your growth — the one thing that, if fixed, unlocks the next stage? Name it, so the rhythm has a target.
10-your-30-60-90-180-day-plan/14-after-1.placeholder: The next constraint is… — I'll know it's eased when…

Your first 90 Days as a Growth Marketer: A Practical Blueprint

11-stages-of-growth/02-idea-and-pre-laun.label: Name the single riskiest assumption your product rests on — the one that, if it's wrong, means nothing else you build matters.
11-stages-of-growth/02-idea-and-pre-laun.placeholder: The riskiest thing I'm assuming is true is… (and I'll know it's true when…)

11-stages-of-growth/03-just-launched.label: Write the one activation number you'll watch this week — the first-value moment, as a count of an action in a time window.
11-stages-of-growth/03-just-launched.placeholder: Activation = [how many] of [key action] within [time window], for a new user. This week it's sitting at roughly…

11-stages-of-growth/04-finding-pmf.label: The 40% PMF test — are you above the bar?
David Rusenko - How To Find Product Market Fit

11-stages-of-growth/05-early-traction.label: Can you actually afford this channel yet?
If I Started SaaS in 2026, Here's My B2B Content Strategy for $1M ARR

11-stages-of-growth/06-scaling.label: Ready to scale? — the signals
items: Retention curve flattens above zero for a segment you can name — the bucket holds, so volume compounds instead of leaking
At least one channel is repeatable with a CAC and payback your model and runway can carry
Unit economics work at target scale — LTV:CAC and payback hold as spend grows, not just at today's small volumes
A weekly growth ritual exists and is actually held — review, scored backlog, decisions citing the funnel
Someone owns the loop end to end (a hat today, a hire as it grows), not five people owning none of it
Core instrumentation is trusted — you'd bet on the activation and retention numbers before you bet money on top of them

11-stages-of-growth/06-scaling.label: Your First 90 Days as Head of Growth (The Exact Plan I Give Every Client)

11-stages-of-growth/07-metrics-that-lie-.label: Key ideas — the metrics that lie by stage

12-running-experiments/01-what-deserves-.label: The ultimate guide to A/B testing

12-running-experiments/02-the-hypothesis.label: Write your next experiment as a hypothesis: We believe [change] for [audience] will cause [outcome], measured by [metric], because [reasoning].
12-running-experiments/02-the-hypothesis.placeholder: We believe … for … will cause … , measured by … , because …

12-running-experiments/03-primary-metric.label: For your next test: the one primary metric that decides it, and the two or three guardrails it isn't allowed to break.

12-running-experiments/07-tempo-and-the-.label: Sean Ellis on why "a high velocity of tests" is crucial for finding product/market fit
The weekly experiment ritual
items: A single backlog exists — every idea written as a hypothesis, ICE-scored
Backlog groomed in a 30-minute weekly growth review
At least one experiment launched or reported every week
Each test names its primary metric and guardrails before it starts
Run length and sample size decided up front — no peeking, no stopping early
Every result recorded: hypothesis, outcome, decision (ship/kill/iterate), what's next
The losers written down too — a flat result is a finding, not a failure

13-talking-about-growth/01-who-cares-abo.label: For each audience — board, cofounder, team, customer — write the one thing they actually want to know about growth this month.

13-talking-about-growth/02-the-two-numbe.label: Write your two-number story: the leading metric you moved, and the lagging outcome it should move — in one sentence that links them.
13-talking-about-growth/02-the-two-numbe.placeholder: We changed …; [leading metric] went from … to …, and [lagging outcome] moved … — because …

13-talking-about-growth/03-reporting-cad.label: Weekly team review — skeleton
items: Metrics read (10 min): North Star vs last week, the retention curve, the one funnel step you're working — is growth real or churn-and-replace?
Journeys and spend (10 min): each live journey's volume and holdout lift; cost per conversion — kill-or-scale decisions made out loud
Experiments (10 min): what finished (result + one-sentence learning), what launches next from the top of the backlog, what's blocked
Every anomaly gets an owner and a next step — not a live debugging session
One learning from the week, written down somewhere permanent

13-talking-about-growth/03-reporting-cad.label: Monthly / board update — skeleton
items: North Star metric: this month's number, and the trend over the last 3–6 months (a line, not a point)
Retention: the curve or the key cohort number — the proof the bucket holds
Lifecycle lift: incremental lift from the global holdout — the number that justifies the program
Acquisition economics: blended CAC, and the share of growth from the owned audience / referral loop
One learning: a single experiment or insight, stated as what you'll do differently — not a list
The one thing you're worried about: for a board, naming a real risk builds more trust than a clean slide

13-talking-about-growth/04-explaining-in.label: Incrementality 101: Everything You Need to Know
Write the plain-language 'how do you know it worked' answer for one of your journeys — the holdout, in words you'd say out loud to a skeptic.
13-talking-about-growth/04-explaining-in.placeholder: Of everyone who qualified, we held back a random …%. The messaged group … ; the held-back group … . The gap — … — is what the journey added.

13-talking-about-growth/05-presenting-a-.label: How to Build a Culture of Experimentation on Your Growth Team
Take one flat or losing result and write it as a paid-for learning: the hypothesis, what happened, and what it rules out or what you'll do next.
13-talking-about-growth/05-presenting-a-.placeholder: We believed … ; the result was … (flat / negative). So the leak isn't … — next we'll test …

13-talking-about-growth/06-vanity-metric.label: Does this number earn its place in the report?
items: So what? — if it were half or double, some decision would change
It's a rate or a cohort, not a cumulative running total
It measures value delivered (active, retained, paid), not reach (impressions, signups, followers)
It can go down — a metric that can only rise can't report a bad month
Someone on the team can name the work that moves it

13-talking-about-growth/06-vanity-metric.label: Take one number you currently report and run the 'so what?' test — then rewrite it as a ratio or a cohort that would actually change a decision.
13-talking-about-growth/06-vanity-metric.placeholder: Vanity: … (cumulative / reach). Actionable version: … (a rate, or split by cohort) — because if it dropped, we'd …

13-talking-about-growth/07-growth-theatr.label: Take one 'X drove Y' attribution claim and rewrite it at the honesty the data supports — separating what you measured from what you're inferring.
13-talking-about-growth/07-growth-theatr.placeholder: Over-claimed: 'X drove Y signups.' Honest version: we measured … ; we think X helped because … ; what we can't separate out is …

13-talking-about-growth/08-the-one-repor.label: The monthly one-pager
items: North Star metric, as a line over 3–6 months (trend, not a point)
Activation rate for recent cohorts + the retention curve or key cohort number
Incremental lift from the lifecycle program (the global holdout) — the number that justifies it
Blended CAC, and the share of growth from the owned audience / referral loop
Exactly one experiment learning, stated as what you'll do differently
One sentence: the risk you're most worried about
Every number is a rate or a trend, not a cumulative total (the §13.6 test)

13-talking-about-growth/08-the-one-repor.label: Draft your one-pager now — fill each block with your real current number, or write 'don't have this yet' where you can't. The gaps are next month's work.
13-talking-about-growth/08-the-one-repor.placeholder:
North Star + trend: …
Activation + retention: …
Blended CAC + referral share: …
One learning: …
The risk I'm worried about: …

13-talking-about-growth/09-before-you-mo.label: One honesty check before the quiz: which audience do you currently over-report to, and which do you under-serve? Name the report you'll change first.
13-talking-about-growth/09-before-you-mo.placeholder: I over-report to … (too much detail / too flattering); I under-serve … . The first report I'll change is …

14-growth-as-career-capital/01-what-its-.label: 10 growth tactics that never work | Elena Verna (Amplitude, Miro, Dropbox, SurveyMonkey)

14-growth-as-career-capital/02-your-work.label: Write the one-paragraph version of your best piece of growth work from this course, in the problem → fix → how-you-knew → next shape. This is the top of your portfolio.
14-growth-as-career-capital/02-your-work.placeholder: In the data I saw… so I shipped… I measured it with… which showed… next I'd…

14-growth-as-career-capital/02-your-work.label: What a strong growth portfolio shows
items: A funnel you instrumented yourself — the events you chose, and why those
At least one journey or experiment you shipped end to end, not just proposed
How you measured impact — a holdout or control, not opens and clicks
One result that didn't work, and what you learned from it
Your reasoning visible at each step, so it reads as judgement not luck
An honest note on scope — real product, practice product, or team effort
Compressed to something readable in two minutes, with detail available underneath

14-growth-as-career-capital/03-project-v.label: Draft one growth story in STAR form. Situation (what the data showed) · Task (what you owned) · Action (what you shipped) · Result (how you measured it, and what you did next). Make the Result about lift against a control, not a raw number.
14-growth-as-career-capital/03-project-v.placeholder: Situation: … · Task: … · Action: … · Result: … and because of it I next…

14-growth-as-career-capital/04-the-90-da.label: Sketch a first-90-days plan for a company you'd want to work at. Days 1–30: what you'd instrument and look at first. Days 31–60: the first fix and how you'd measure it. Days 61–90: what a first win would gate. Keep it about learning before acting.
14-growth-as-career-capital/04-the-90-da.placeholder: Company: … · 1–30 (measure): … · 31–60 (first fix + holdout): … · 61–90 (what a win unlocks): …

14-growth-as-career-capital/05-interview.label: Growth interview prep
items: Rehearse a North Star answer for their product — value-reflecting, not a vanity metric
Have one STAR experiment story ready, landed on lift against a control
Sign up as a real user beforehand and note where the funnel leaks
Prepare a first-90-days sketch aimed at their business (measure before you act)
Be ready to say 'here's how I'd know it worked' for any claim you make
Bring your workbook/portfolio one-pager as concrete evidence
Have operator questions ready: data access, who owns the loop, what's been tried

14-growth-as-career-capital/05-interview.label: Draft three questions you'll ask a growth interviewer — the ones that reveal whether the role is real and whether you'd have what you need to do the job well.
14-growth-as-career-capital/05-interview.placeholder: 1. Is the product instrumented, and will I have data access? 2. Who owns the growth loop today? 3. …

14-growth-as-career-capital/06-reading-a.label: Key ideas — reading a growth role

14-growth-as-career-capital/07-attributi.label: Write one impact claim twice: first the way you'd be tempted to say it, then the honest version — pairing the result with how you measured it and what you're still unsure about. Keep the second one.
14-growth-as-career-capital/07-attributi.placeholder: Tempted: 'I grew X by …' → Honest: 'We shipped … and measured it with a holdout; the treated group … I'd want … before calling it durable.'

14-growth-as-career-capital/08-build-you.label: Plan your first owned-audience move. One thing you'll write or share about the growth work you're doing — the topic, where you'll put it, and by when. Small and real beats big and someday.
14-growth-as-career-capital/08-build-you.placeholder: I'll write about … (e.g. what my onboarding holdout taught me), publish it on … by …

14-growth-as-career-capital/08-build-you.label: The Ultimate Guide to Developer Marketing | Lee Robinson (Vercel)
